title: raouliopsis pachymorpha
intro: Raouliopsis pachymorpha, also known as the "tree daisy," is a succulent plant species native to the island of Hawaii. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is valued for its potential medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Raouliopsis pachymorpha can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which may be used to support skin health and other health concerns.
